phase_description: Phase 4 active — first candidate delivered. All v0.3.2 content plus: Wikipedia 'Kangxi radicals' source pinned at revision 1346511063 via index.php action=raw (CC-BY-SA 4.0); new wikitable parser in build/transform/radicals.py extracting 214 classical radicals + 46 documented alternate forms; data/core/radicals.json now has 197 of 253 radicals (77.9%) populated with authoritative English meanings and Kangxi numbers (previously all empty); global User-Agent header added to build/fetch.py per Wikipedia's UA policy; schemas/radical.schema.json bumped to 0.4.0; regression test for Wikipedia ingestion added; trailing-edge cleanup: CHANGELOG [0.1.0] and [0.2.0] updated with rows/unique split to match [0.3.0], CI workflow summary message no longer has stale hardcoded test count. 57/57 tests passing. Remaining 56 radicals (22.1%) are Japanese-dictionary-specific variant forms whose Wikipedia mapping requires a curated alias table, deferred to v0.4.x.

jmdict-examples-eng.description: JMdict English entries with Tatoeba example sentences pre-linked.

kanjivg.description: KanjiVG stroke order SVG files (non-variant forms).

kanjium-accents.description: Kanjium pitch accent TSV (word, reading, mora positions).

kanjidic2.description: KANJIDIC2 kanji data, all languages (13,108 characters, full coverage).

waller-jlpt-vocab-n5.description: Jonathan Waller JLPT N5 vocabulary list (CSV) via stephenmk/yomitan-jlpt-vocab.

waller-jlpt-vocab-n4.description: Jonathan Waller JLPT N4 vocabulary list (CSV) via stephenmk/yomitan-jlpt-vocab.

waller-jlpt-vocab-n3.description: Jonathan Waller JLPT N3 vocabulary list (CSV) via stephenmk/yomitan-jlpt-vocab.

waller-jlpt-vocab-n2.description: Jonathan Waller JLPT N2 vocabulary list (CSV) via stephenmk/yomitan-jlpt-vocab.

waller-jlpt-vocab-n1.description: Jonathan Waller JLPT N1 vocabulary list (CSV) via stephenmk/yomitan-jlpt-vocab.

waller-jlpt-kanji.description: Jonathan Waller JLPT kanji classifications via davidluzgouveia/kanji-data (jlpt_new field only). Code is MIT; data fields derive from Waller CC-BY.

wikipedia-kangxi-radicals.description: Wikipedia 'Kangxi radicals' article raw wikitext, pinned to revision 1346511063 (2024). Source for radical English meanings and Kangxi numbers used to populate data/core/radicals.json.

grammar_curation_status.note: Zero native-speaker reviewers engaged as of this release. docs/contributing.md has the aspirational call for reviewers but no operational pipeline exists yet. The grammar dataset is usable-but-not-authoritative until this changes. This is the single weakest dimension of the project by the maintainer's own admission in README.md and docs/gaps.md.

next_actions: Grammar: expand N3 coverage from the current batch 1 (40 entries) toward ~80-100 entries across successive patch releases; tag v0.5.0 when N3 reaches a stable milestone. Continue filling remaining N4 gaps — N4 is at 36 entries against community-standard ~80-100 for a 'complete' N4 grammar set.
Recruit native-speaker reviewers to move grammar entries from draft → native_speaker_reviewed (see docs/contributing.md).

Text-match Tatoeba sentences against grammar-curated examples to populate Tatoeba sentence IDs where exact matches exist (currently ~1.7% link rate on 180 original examples; expanded corpus brings new linkage opportunities).

Phase 4: evaluate remaining candidates in docs/phase4-candidates.md — notably JPDB modern frequency (blocked on license clarification), variant-to-Kangxi radical alias table (~22% radical gap), post-2022 Wiktionary pitch accent fill, classical handwriting datasets, and audio corpora.
